scope_under_review.what_it_proposes: Add a typed link between public.agent_tasks rows and the rich task descriptors held inside agent_architectures.tasks jsonb, so Builder/Verification/Router agents can join from a flat task row to its decomposition entry without re-implementing the bridge per consumer.
scope_under_review.claimed_gap: Architecture Agent (n8n workflow 5V9uqEVLxJBbtJEr) decomposes specs into agent_architectures.tasks jsonb array with task_id/name/description/inputs/outputs/dependencies/estimated_hours/component fields, but public.agent_tasks is flat and the Builder bridges manually via spec_reference matching.
claude_observations_to_check: Scope file status is 'Draft — pending codex review and operator approval' — confirm review can proceed
Scope mentions concrete in-flight Builder task `641341d9-cb61-4cde-9db3-55cb5e0c5bd9` — verify this is current state, not stale
Scope is dated 2026-05-25 — today is 2026-05-30; verify nothing has shifted in the architecture/builder pipeline since
Scope is read-only per its own author note — no migrations attempted from the scope itself

review_questions: Is the diagnosed gap real and persistent, or has it been mitigated by a downstream agent change since 2026-05-25?
Does the proposed schema fix conflict with any approved tool or workflow contract (e.g. n8n workflows that read agent_tasks directly)?
Would adopting the proposal create downstream migration debt for agent_architectures, agent_routing_logs, agent_build_artifacts, or agent_verifications?
Is the scope spec-complexity-ceiling compliant per PT9 (≤30 lines for identity/inputs/outputs/DoD), or does it need to be split before promotion?
Are there security implications (RLS, service-role exposure, tenant boundaries) the scope underweights?
What is the smallest safe migration to close the gap if accepted?

files: Codex Task Result (severity-classified findings + verdict + recommended next packet shape)
expected_output.report: VERDICT: one of ACCEPT-AS-SCOPE (Claude promotes to SPEC under PT9 constraints), REVISE (counter-proposal + iteration loop via claude_codex_loop if needed), REJECT (close as out-of-scope/premature with rationale). Include: severity-classified findings list; mapping of each finding to a doctrine rule (R15/R16/R19/PT9 etc.); concrete next-packet shape if ACCEPT or REVISE.

success_criteria: Verdict reached and recorded in this packet's status field.
Codex Task Result file exists and is linked from the verdict.
If ACCEPT-AS-SCOPE: a follow-on packet is queued for Claude to promote the scope to a PT9-compliant SPEC.
If REVISE: counter-proposal cites specific findings + suggests scope-doc edits OR a different approach.
If REJECT: rationale references concrete doctrine rules and/or contradicting current architecture state.
rollback_plan: If review cannot complete: leave SCOPE-AGEN-AGENT-TASKS-SCHEMA-FIX-v1.md untracked in working tree (its current state). Append BLOCKED status + reason to this packet. Do not delete the scope file.
